m_Item1.QuestionText: What is the full name of JSP?
m_Item1.Explanation: JSP - Java Server Pages

m_Item1.QuestionText:
If you want to override a JSP file's initialization method, within what type of tags must you declare the
method?
m_Item1.Explanation:
<%!  %>    --  declaration - can be used to declare a method used for initialization.
 <%   %>   --   java code
 <%= %>   --   expression

m_Item1.QuestionText: How can a servlet call a JSP error page?

Options.OptionText: When the servlet throws the exception, it will automatically be caught by the calling JSP page.
The servlet needs to forward the request to the specific error page in JSP which uses errorPage attribute in its page directive
The servlet needs to redirect the response to the specific error page, saving the exception off in a cookie.

m_Item1.Explanation:
Exception Handling in JSP :
The servlet needs to forward the request to the specific error page URL. The exception is passed along as an attribute named "javax.servlet.jsp.jspException” .

m_Item1.QuestionText: Choose the statement that best describes the relationship between JSP and servlets:

Options.OptionText: Servlets are built on JSP semantics and all servlets are compiled to JSP pages for runtime usage.
JSP and servlets are unrelated technologies.
Servlets and JSP are competing technologies for handling web requests. Servlets are being superseded by JSP, which is preferred. The two technologies are not useful in combination.
JSPs are built on servlet semantics and all JSPs are compiled to servlets for runtime usage.

m_Item1.Explanation: JSPs are based on servlets So all jsp pages are need to translated & compiled  into servlet for runtime usage.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Two of the following methods can be overridden in jsp page
m_Item1.Explanation:
Methods which overridden in jsp page :
                            jspInit()
                            _jspService()
                            jspDestroy()

m_Item1.QuestionText: One of the following is jsp expression
m_Item1.Explanation: <%= %> is a JSP expression.

m_Item1.QuestionText: One of the following is not an implicit jsp object
m_Item1.Explanation:
Implicit JSP Objects  :
                        request
                        response
                        out
                        config
                        session
                        pageContext
                        page
                        exception
                        application

m_Item1.QuestionText: One of the following is an additional attribute in Jsp as compare to servlet attributes .
m_Item1.Explanation: Page level attributes in JSP are additional as compared to servlets.

m_Item1.QuestionText: When jsp is generated into servlet it is derived from _________ class
m_Item1.Explanation: public abstract class HttpJspBase which extends from javax.servlet.http.HttpServlet and also implements javax.servlet.jsp.HttpJspPage.From this class jsp generated servlets are derived.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Pure java code can be written inside
m_Item1.Explanation: Pure java code can be written inside JSP scriptlet on JSP page.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the attribute of JSP mentioned below is not available in servlet?
m_Item1.Explanation: page is the attribute of JSP which is not available in servlet.

m_Item1.QuestionText: A method can be defined inside
m_Item1.Explanation: Method can be defined in JSP declaration.

m_Item1.QuestionText: One of the following cannot be overridden while writing JSP page
m_Item1.Explanation:
JSP life cycle methods are :
                         jspInit
                         jspService
                         JspDestroy
_jspService  method can not be overridden while writing JSP page.

m_Item1.QuestionText: When a JSP page is compiled, what is it turned into?
m_Item1.Explanation: After compilation of JSP page is turned into Servlet.

m_Item1.QuestionText:
If a JSP page overrides the jsplnit() method using a declaration JSP tag, which phase of the JSP page
life-cycle generates the overridden method in the servlet?
m_Item1.Explanation: Page translation phase of JSP page life cycle generates the overridden method in the servlet.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which method can be overridden while writing JSP page
m_Item1.Explanation: jspInit() and jspDestroy() methods can be overridden in JSP Page.

m_Item1.QuestionText: The implicit object “out” of Jsp is of type
m_Item1.Explanation: JspWriter's implicit object out is of type Jsp.

m_Item1.QuestionText: JSP invokes the  _______ method before servicing any requests
m_Item1.Explanation: JSP method jspInit() is automatically called by the Server when a JSP page is initialized or executed for the first time. This method has a public access-modifier and it doesn't accept nor returns any value. In this method, we can add code some initialization code. For example, it could be initializing an object to a value or we could initialize objects to values or we can open a database connection.

m_Item1.QuestionText: If a jsp is to generate response as xml page, what attribute of page directive it should use?

Options.OptionText: no page directive attribute needs to be used

m_Item1.Explanation: contentType attribute of page directive used to generate response as xml page.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What programming language(s) or scripting language(s) does Java Server Pages (JSP) support?
m_Item1.Explanation: Java only the programming language supports Java Server Pages.

m_Item1.QuestionText:
Which of the following is/are true for JSP technology ?
I. It is persistent.
II. Platform independent.
III. Browser executes the code.
m_Item1.Explanation: JSP does not get executed on the browser. So option III is not correct. JSP itself is not persistent

m_Item1.QuestionText: How many copies of a JSP page can be in memory at a time?
m_Item1.Explanation: Only one copy of a JSP page can be in memory at a time

m_Item1.QuestionText: What alternatives exist to embedding Java code directly within the HTML markup of your JSP page?

Options.OptionText: Moving the code into your session manager.
Moving the code into JavaBeans and servlets
Moving the code into a transaction manager

m_Item1.Explanation: On JSP page, java code needs to move into scriptlets.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following scripting elements can be used to declare methods and fields?
m_Item1.Explanation: <%! ... > allows to declare methods and fields (instance members) in generated servlet class of JSP

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is the initial contact point for handling a web request in a Page-Centric architecture?
m_Item1.Explanation: Unlike the Model-View-Controller (MVC) pattern, Page-Centric architecture doesn't involve a separate controller component. The JSP page takes on the responsibility of handling the request directly.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which one is the correct order of phases in JSP life cycle?
m_Item1.Explanation: The correct order is Compilation, Initialization, Execution, Cleanup.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Can <%--comment--%> be used in JSP?
m_Item1.Explanation: <!--comment--> is an HTML comment. <%--comment--%> is JSP comment.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What does JSP stand for in the context of Java web technology?
m_Item1.Explanation: JSP stands for Java Server Pages, a technology used for developing webpages that support dynamic content.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is the primary advantage of using JSP over servlets?

Options.OptionText: JSP separates presentation from business logic.
JSP is not dependent on Java.

m_Item1.Explanation: JSP allows developers to separate the presentation layer from the business logic, making the development process cleaner and more manageable.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following is NOT a valid JSP implicit object?
m_Item1.Explanation: The 'connection' object is not an implicit object in JSP. JSP provides several implicit objects like application, config, session, etc.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of these correctly describes the lifecycle method of JSP for initialization?
m_Item1.Explanation: jspInit() is the lifecycle method called to initialize the JSP page.

m_Item1.QuestionText: In JSP, what is the role of the 'useBean' tag?

Options.OptionText: It forwards request to another page.
It declares a JavaBean for use in a JSP.

m_Item1.Explanation: <jsp:useBean> is used to declare a JavaBean in a JSP page.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Where do JSP expressions get evaluated?

Options.OptionText: At the time of compiling the JSP
At the time of server startup
At runtime, when the request is made

m_Item1.Explanation: JSP expressions are evaluated at runtime when the request is made to render the dynamic content.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is the default extension for a JSP file?
m_Item1.Explanation: The default extension for a JSP file is .jsp indicating that it is a Java Server Page.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which phase of the JSP life cycle is responsible for converting the JSP page into a servlet?
m_Item1.Explanation: During the translation phase, the JSP engine converts the JSP page into a servlet. This is the first step in the JSP life cycle.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is the purpose of the 'jspInit()' method in the JSP life cycle?

Options.OptionText: To compile the JSP page into a Java servlet
To initialize resources before processing requests

m_Item1.Explanation: The 'jspInit()' method is called only once when the JSP page is initialized. It is used to perform any initialization tasks before handling client requests.

m_Item1.QuestionText: In which phase is 'jspDestroy()' invoked?
m_Item1.Explanation: The 'jspDestroy()' method is invoked during the cleanup phase. It is called once when the JSP page is about to be destroyed, allowing for resource cleanup.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which method is automatically called to handle each client request in the JSP life cycle?
m_Item1.Explanation: The '_jspService()' method is called automatically to process each client request. It is generated by the JSP engine and should not be overridden by developers.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is the primary role of the JSP container in the life cycle process?

Options.OptionText: To generate dynamic content and update the HTML
To manage the translation and execution of JSP pages
To compile and package Java applications

m_Item1.Explanation: The JSP container manages the translation, compilation, and execution of JSP pages as part of the JSP life cycle.

m_Item1.QuestionText: How can a JSP developer explicitly declare initialization parameters for their JSP?

Options.OptionText: By using JavaScript within the JSP
By defining context parameters in web.xml
By calling constructors in JSP directly

m_Item1.Explanation: Developers can declare initialization parameters through the deployment descriptor file, web.xml, which the JSP container reads and provides for initialization during the jspInit() phase.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Why is it critical for JSP developers to understand the JSP life cycle phases?

Options.OptionText: It ensures that the JSP page compiles without errors
It helps in writing faster JavaScript code
It allows creating efficient resource management strategies
It confines the use of JSP to server-side scripting only

m_Item1.Explanation: Understanding the JSP life cycle allows developers to manage resources efficiently, such as opening and closing connections and releasing memory, improving the performance and reliability of web applications.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is the primary function of a JSP tag library?

Options.OptionText: To compile JSP files into Java servlet code
To provide reusable components and custom tags in JSP
To manage session data in JSP applications
To handle database connections in JSP

m_Item1.Explanation: JSP tag libraries are used to extend the capabilities of JSP pages by providing reusable components and custom tags, which simplify the development process.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following directives is used to include another file into a JSP page?
m_Item1.Explanation: The <%@ include %> directive allows static inclusion of a file during the translation phase, which effectively inserts the contents of the file into the JSP.

m_Item1.QuestionText: In JSP, which implicit object is NOT common between JSP pages and servlets?
m_Item1.Explanation: The 'page' implicit object is specific to JSP and refers to the instance of the generated servlet itself, which is not applicable in servlets.

m_Item1.QuestionText: How can you declare a scripting element in JSP to initialize a variable?
m_Item1.Explanation: <%! %> is used to declare variables and methods in JSP which can be later initialized or used within the scriptlet.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which JSP directive is used to define the attributes that can be passed to the JSP page as name-value pairs?
m_Item1.Explanation: The 'page' directive is used to provide global attributes to the JSP page, such as content type, language, and buffer size.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which element in JSP should be used to insert dynamic Java expressions directly into the output?
m_Item1.Explanation: <%= %> is used to evaluate Java expressions and include the results directly into the output stream sent to the client.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which implicit object is used to access HTTP headers sent by the client?
m_Item1.Explanation: The 'request' implicit object is used to access all the data sent by the client, including HTTP headers.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following statements about the 'session' implicit object is true?

Options.OptionText: It allows sharing data among all users.
It's available for the entire application lifecycle.
It maintains user data across multiple requests.

m_Item1.Explanation: The 'session' implicit object maintains user-specific data across multiple requests, enabling session management.

m_Item1.QuestionText: How can you use the 'out' implicit object in JSP?

Options.OptionText: To generate and send output to the client browser.

m_Item1.Explanation: The 'out' implicit object in JSP is an instance of 'JspWriter' and is used to send data to the client browser.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What role does the 'exception' implicit object play in JSP?

Options.OptionText: It provides access to shared resources across sessions.
It contains exception data when a JSP error page is invoked.
It facilitates communication between JSP and servlet.

m_Item1.Explanation: The 'exception' implicit object is available only in JSP error pages and contains information about the exception that was thrown.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What happens if jspDestroy() is overridden in JSP?

Options.OptionText: It is ignored by the container
It will be called on every request
It is called once when JSP is taken out of service

m_Item1.Explanation:
jspDestroy() is like the destroy() method in servlets.
It's called once, before the JSP is unloaded (e.g., during redeployment or server shutdown).
It's a good place to release resources.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which type of comment in JSP gets sent to the client/browser as part of the response?
m_Item1.Explanation:
HTML comments are part of the JSP output and are visible in the page source in the browser. 
JSP comments are removed at translation time and are not visible in the response.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following is NOT a valid difference between JSP and Servlet?

Options.OptionText: JSP is used for UI; Servlet for logic
Servlet is HTML inside Java; JSP is Java inside HTML
JSP must be manually compiled; Servlet is auto-compiled
JSP can use custom tags and EL; Servlets can’t directly use them

m_Item1.Explanation:
Both JSP and Servlet are auto-compiled by the server (e.g., Tomcat).
JSP is translated into servlet .java file, then compiled into .class.
You don’t need to manually compile either in modern environments.
